title: festuca rubra subsp. kitaibeliana
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. kitaibeliana, also known as red fescue, is a subspecies of grass native to Central and Eastern Europe, characterized by its reddish or purple tint on the leaves. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its high content of phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Festuca rubra subsp. kitaibeliana include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and acting as an antiseptic, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
